DISCOVERY 610, APPEARANCES: SANFORD MICHAEL CIPINKO, FOR PLAINTIFF AND MOVING PARTY: JAMES MILLER JR. CHRISTOPHER F. JOHNSON FOR DEFENDANTS AND OPPOSING PARTY: SBC COMMUNICATIONS IP INC. , SBC ADVANCED SOLUTIONS, INC. AND JOAQUIN MEDINA ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL PRODUCTION OF RECORDS FROM DEFENDANT IS DENIED. SINCE THE DEPOSITION SUBPOENA WAS SERVED UPON A NON-PARTY FOR RECORDS IN THEIR CUSTODY OR CONTROL, THE DEFENDANTS ARE NOT THE PROPER PARTIES TO THE MOTION. COMM: BRUCE E. CHAN

DISCOVERY 610, APPEARANCES: CHRISTOPHER F. JOHNSON FOR DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY: SBC ADVANCED SOLUTIONS INC. AND JOAQUIN MEDINA SANFORD MICHAEL CIPINKO FOR PLAINTIFF AND OPPOSING PARTY: JAMES MILLER JR. ARGUED AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT MEDINA'S MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS IS GRANTED. THE SUBJECT MATTER OF THE SUBPOENAS IS IRRELEVANT TO THE SUBJECT MATTER OF THIS ACTION AS DEFENDANT HAS OFFERED TO STIPULATE THAT THE ONLY ISSUE AT TRIAL IS DAMAGES, AND THEY WILL NEITHER ASSERT COMPARATIVE NEGLIGENCE ON THE PART OF THE DECEDENT NOR CLAIM THAT ANY THIRD PARTY WAS A SUBSTANTIAL FACTOR IN CAUSING DEATH. IN LIGHT OF THE PROPOSED STIPULATION, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED GOOD CAUSE TO OVERCOME THE PRIVACY INTEREST IN THE SUBPOENAED MEDICAL RECORDS. COMM: BRUCE E. CHAN
